summary: Design voice message + slideshow story types via judge panel of 3 design lenses

tradeoffs: Reuse the `images` bucket for audio instead of a new `audio` bucket: saves a migration + RLS policies + the cartoon-style folder-scoped insert policies; the only cost is mixed media in one bucket, mitigated by the audio/ path prefix. Verified the bucket has no MIME allowlist in code, so audio/* uploads succeed.

Store durationSec in content captured at record/upload time rather than reading it in the viewer: webm blobs from MediaRecorder report duration=Infinity in <audio> on Chrome, so trusting the element is unreliable. Capturing it once (elapsed timer for recordings, loadedmetadata for uploads) is simpler and correct everywhere, and lets the player render the timeline immediately.

Model SlideshowCard on ScratchCard's exact prop shape (initialRevealed/onComplete) and treat 'reached last slide' as the reveal event: this lets the slideshow plug into the page's existing revealedStories/shouldAutoAdvance machine with a ONE-LINE predicate add — no new page state, identical UX contract to scratch.

Treat 'voice' like 'youtube' for auto-advance (excluded entirely) instead of inventing a 'play-to-completion-then-advance' flow: a 5s timer would cut off a voice message, and matching youtube's existing 'user taps Next' behavior is the least code and most predictable. One-line add to shouldAutoAdvance.

Upload audio blob verbatim (no transcode) and upload slideshow images one-by-one via the existing uploadImage: avoids ffmpeg.wasm / any new dependency and reuses the WebP pipeline 100%. Cost: slideshow upload is sequential (<=5 small files, negligible) and audio stays in its native container (webm/mp4/mp3/wav), which all modern mobile browsers play via the right <source type>.

One shared VoicePlayer component for both the editor preview and the viewer: guarantees WYSIWYG and halves the surface area, at the cost of the player needing a couple of presentational props (caption, autoFocus). Worth it for maintainability.
Slide progress shown via dots, story progress via the existing segmented top bar — keep the two progress concepts separate so the established progress-bar code is untouched.

No audio transcoding: we store the raw MediaRecorder container (Chrome audio/webm-opus, Safari audio/mp4-aac) and play it back per-platform via a correctly-typed <source>. Avoids ffmpeg/wasm and any npm dependency. Trade-off: a webm-opus file recorded on Chrome and opened on a very old iOS Safari could fail to decode; mitigated because each device plays back the format it can record, modern browsers cross-decode opus/aac, and uploaded mp3/m4a is universally safe.

Static seeded waveform as the guaranteed baseline (deterministic from audioUrl) instead of always decoding audio with Web Audio API. It looks like a real voiceprint, needs zero decode, and works during the no-gesture window. A true AnalyserNode-driven live waveform is offered as a progressive enhancement on first play. Trade-off: baseline bars don't reflect actual amplitude.

New `audio` bucket for voice but reuse `images` for slideshow. Slideshow is literally N images, so the existing WebP pipeline applies unchanged; audio gets isolated lifecycle, mime whitelist, and size cap. Trade-off: one more bucket to provision, but it cleanly mirrors the cartoon-images precedent.

Voice and slideshow both follow the scratch/youtube auto-advance contract rather than inventing new flow: voice is excluded from the 5s timer (never cut off mid-sentence) and can reveal-on-end; slideshow blocks story auto-advance until the album finishes, then hands off to the existing 5s segmented-bar advance. Consistent recipient mental model, minimal page.tsx change.

Reorder via left/right chevron buttons (matching StoryList's up/down arrows) instead of drag-and-drop. No dnd library, touch-friendly, consistent with the codebase. Trade-off: slightly less slick than dragging for 5 items, but far simpler and dependency-free.
Minimum 2 images for slideshow (1 image is steered to the simpler 'image' type). Keeps the slideshow meaningful and the Ken Burns/dots UI non-degenerate.
Store durationSec at creation time so the viewer's player and progress fill are correct instantly, even before audio metadata loads on slow networks — avoids a jarring UI jump.

Store ONLY public URLs + plain metadata in stories.content (never blobs) — matches the opaque `content: Json` contract (storage.ts:127) so saveMemory's delete-and-reinsert needs zero changes, and stays JSON-serializable.

durationSec is computed editor-side from a wall-clock timer, not trusted from MediaRecorder — sidesteps the well-known Chrome webm `duration: Infinity` bug so the viewer never renders NaN; the viewer reconciles to real metadata only if finite.

Voice + slideshow plug into the EXISTING reveal→auto-advance contract (scratch/question pattern at page.tsx:149-164) rather than inventing new lifecycle: voice reveals onended, slideshow reveals on last slide; both then ride the standard 5s timer. Minimal, proven surface area.

New dedicated 'audio' bucket vs reusing 'images': chosen new bucket for clean RLS/MIME/lifecycle and simpler future GC; slideshow images reuse 'images'+uploadImage WebP pipeline because they ARE images and benefit from existing compression.
Reorder via chevron ◀▶ buttons (reusing StoryList.tsx:49-69 swap logic) over HTML5 drag-and-drop — far more reliable on touch for the Thai mobile-first audience and accessible by default.

Uploads happen at SUBMIT only, with per-slide success caching, so a partial failure (3/5) retries only the failed images and never writes a half-list to the DB — at the cost of accepting the same orphaned-object risk the existing single-image flow already has (deferred to a future GC cron).
Bounded-concurrency (2) slideshow upload instead of 5-parallel — trades a little speed for resilience on slow/unstable Thai mobile networks.

No-autoplay, gesture-gated playback for voice (the Play button IS the gesture) — complies with iOS/Chrome autoplay policy AND avoids a surprise blasting audio unexpectedly; the cost is the recipient must tap to hear, which we make obvious with a large themed button.

finalSpec.summary:
FINAL DESIGN — base is the ROBUSTNESS design (unanimous judge winner: best codebase fit, best failure-mode coverage, correct migration number, graceful degradation), with the highest-value grafts from the SIMPLICITY and UX-POLISH designs resolved against the actual code. Verified against the real files: next migration is 021 (020 is latest; 018 was already taken — simplicity's number was a collision); upload.ts imports `{ supabase }` from './supabase' and uploadImage hardcodes contentType 'image/webp'+'images/' prefix; StoryViewer's generic onReveal is `() => setRevealedStories(prev => new Set(prev).add(id))` and ScratchCard's contract is `{ initialRevealed, onComplete }`; getStoryPreview's default returns ''; shouldAutoAdvance (page.tsx:149-153) excludes youtube outright and gates scratch on reveal; handleContentTap skip-list (page.tsx:173) already covers button/canvas/[data-interactive]; the cartoon-images bucket (012) is public + folder-scoped authenticated INSERT + service-role ALL and has NO file_size_limit/allowed_mime_types.

Two new story types: VOICE (record-or-upload audio ≤60s, ≤10MB, no-autoplay gesture-gated player) and SLIDESHOW (2–5 images, Ken Burns auto-play, swipe/tap/dots). Both persist only public URLs + plain metadata in the existing opaque `content` jsonb (saveMemory needs zero change). Storage: NEW dedicated public `audio` bucket mirroring the cartoon-images precedent (sidesteps the unverifiable images-bucket-MIME gamble that simplicity made); slideshow reuses the existing `images` bucket via uploadImage. Grafts adopted: shared VoicePlayer used by BOTH editor preview and viewer (WYSIWYG, from simplicity/ux-polish); SlideshowViewer adopts ScratchCard's exact `{ initialRevealed, onComplete }` prop contract so it plugs into page.tsx:455 onReveal with ZERO new page state; getStoryPreview cases added (from simplicity); read blob.type AFTER recorder.stop() for the Safari mp4 fix (from ux-polish); large gradient play orb with playing-only pulse aura + static seeded waveform (ux-polish emotional layer on robustness's safe state machine — NO live AnalyserNode during recording, NO AudioContext); Ken Burns via 3 CSS keyframe variants cycled index%3 with prefers-reduced-motion; per-slide top segmented mini-progress + dots; 'กำลังอัปโหลด n/N' counter with bounded-concurrency(2) per-slide success caching; celebratory dot pulse on last slide; SLIDESHOW_MIN_IMAGES=2 (steer 1 image to 'image' type).

Conflict resolutions: (1) VOICE auto-advance — adopt the SIMPLER youtube-style exclusion (voice never auto-advances; recipient taps Next) over ux-polish's reveal-on-end+900ms-handleNext, because onended is flaky on iOS mid-stream and the youtube pattern is a proven one-line change requiring no new page state. (2) Bucket strategy — NEW audio bucket (robustness/ux-polish) NOT images reuse (simplicity), because the images bucket's RLS INSERT policy is dashboard-created and unverifiable from code; a dedicated bucket with its own voices/ folder policy is the only safe choice. (3) Audio bucket migration includes file_size_limit + allowed_mime_types but mime allowlist is OMITTED-by-default-commented because a codec-suffixed 'audio/mp4;codecs=...' or empty-MIME iOS file can be wrongly rejected by the bucket allowlist; the editor-side 60s/10MB checks are the real gate. (4) Reorder via ◀▶ chevron buttons (StoryList pattern) not drag-and-drop.

voiceSpec.editorSpec:
PLACEMENT: extend the EXISTING src/components/StoryEditor.tsx switch — no new editor shell. The voice UI body lives in a small co-located subcomponent `VoiceRecorderField` rendered inside StoryEditor when type==='voice' (keeps the recorder state machine isolated from the giant form, while wiring through the same registry + handleSubmit). Import { Mic } from 'lucide-react' (already imports from lucide-react).

REGISTRY ADDITIONS (StoryEditor.tsx lines 29-57):
- storyTypeLabels.voice = 'ข้อความเสียง'
- storyTypeDescriptions.voice = 'บันทึกเสียงหรืออัปโหลดไฟล์เสียง สูงสุด 1 นาที'
- storyTypeIcons.voice = Mic
(These three additions auto-wire the type-selector grid at line 308 AND StoryList icon/label at lines 103/111 with no extra code.)

NEW STATE (alongside existing useState block ~line 123):
  audioUrl: string                // getInitialAudioUrl() — existing saved URL when editing
  audioBlob: Blob | null          // freshly recorded/picked, pre-upload
  audioPreviewUrl: string         // object URL for the shared VoicePlayer preview
  audioDuration: number
  audioMime: string
  audioSource: 'record' | 'upload'
  voiceCaption: string            // getInitialVoiceCaption()
  recMode: 'idle' | 'requesting' | 'recording' | 'recorded' | 'denied' | 'unsupported'
  recSeconds: number              // live elapsed during recording
REFS: mediaRecorderRef, mediaStreamRef (for explicit track.stop()), chunksRef (Blob[]), recTimerRef (interval id), startTimeRef, audioObjectUrlRef (mirror the existing objectUrlRef cleanup at StoryEditor.tsx:139-148 — add a SECOND ref for audio).

INITIALIZERS (mirror getInitialImageUrl/getInitialCaption pattern):
  getInitialAudioUrl(): editingStory?.type==='voice' ? content.audioUrl : ''
  getInitialVoiceCaption(): editingStory?.type==='voice' ? content.caption||'' : ''
  (also seed audioDuration/audioMime from editingStory.content when editing so the preview player renders correctly.)

CAPABILITY DETECTION (useEffect on mount, only when type can be voice): if `typeof MediaRecorder === 'undefined' || !navigator.mediaDevices?.getUserMedia` → setRecMode('unsupported'): HIDE the record orb, show ONLY the upload input, with note 'อุปกรณ์นี้ไม่รองรับการอัดเสียง กรุณาอัปโหลดไฟล์เสียงแทน'. CRITICAL for Thai traffic: covers old iOS AND LINE/Facebook/Messenger in-app webviews. Upload path works everywhere.

MIME SELECTION fn (the Safari↔Chrome crux):
  function pickAudioMime(): string {
    const PREFS = ['audio/mp4','audio/webm;codecs=opus','audio/webm','audio/mpeg','audio/ogg;codecs=opus'];
    for (const t of PREFS) if (MediaRecorder.isTypeSupported?.(t)) return t;
    return ''; // '' => let the browser default rather than throw in the constructor
  }
  Note: iOS Safari yields audio/mp4(AAC), Chrome/Android audio/webm;opus. We store the ACTUAL blob.type after stop, never the requested string.

RECORD FLOW:
  1. recMode 'idle': a large circular GLOWING record orb (88px, gradient `linear-gradient(135deg, ${primary}, ${dark})`, white Mic icon, animate-pulse-heart ring). Below it a secondary text button 'หรืออัปโหลดไฟล์เสียง'.
  2. Tap orb → setRecMode('requesting') → navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({audio:true}). Error handling: NotAllowedError/SecurityError → setRecMode('denied'); NotFoundError → showToast('ไม่พบไมโครโฟน','error') + back to idle; any other → showToast('ไม่สามารถเข้าถึงไมโครโฟนได้','error') + idle.
  3. On grant: new MediaRecorder(stream, picked ? {mimeType:picked} : undefined); ondataavailable→chunksRef.push(e.data); start(1000) (timeslice gets chunks even if tab backgrounds). setRecMode('recording'); startTimeRef=Date.now(); recTimer = setInterval(200ms) → setRecSeconds(Math.round((Date.now()-startTimeRef)/1000)). UI: orb morphs into a red stop square with running 'M:SS / 1:00' (aria-live=polite) and a thin themed progress bar toward 60s. Use the static seeded waveform component (see viewerSpec) in a 'recording' visual variant — bars rendered but NOT amplitude-reactive (no AudioContext, deliberately).
  4. HARD CAP: when recSeconds >= 60 → auto-call stop() + showToast('อัดเสียงได้สูงสุด 1 นาที','info').
  5. Tap 'หยุด' → recorder.stop(). onstop handler: const blob = new Blob(chunksRef.current, {type: recorder.mimeType || picked || 'audio/webm'}); setAudioBlob(blob); setAudioMime(blob.type); setAudioSource('record'); audioPreviewUrl = URL.createObjectURL(blob) (store in audioObjectUrlRef, revoke prior); durationSec = Math.min(60, Math.max(1, Math.round((Date.now()-startTimeRef)/1000))) [wall-clock — DO NOT trust audioEl.duration for webm]; setRecMode('recorded'). CLEANUP: mediaStreamRef.current.getTracks().forEach(t=>t.stop()) [release mic / kill red indicator]; clearInterval(recTimerRef.current).

UPLOAD-FILE FLOW (always available):
  <input type='file' accept='audio/*,.m4a,.mp3,.wav,.aac,.ogg,.webm'> styled with the same file: classes as the image input (StoryEditor.tsx:497). onChange:
  - size <= VOICE_MAX_BYTES else showToast('ไฟล์เสียงใหญ่เกินไป (สูงสุด 10MB)','error').
  - acceptance: isAcceptableAudio(file) = file.type.startsWith('audio/') || /\.(mp3|m4a|wav|aac|ogg|webm)$/i.test(file.name) [extension fallback MANDATORY — iOS often gives empty file.type]; else showToast('ไฟล์นี้ไม่ใช่ไฟล์เสียง','error').
  - duration probe: const a = new Audio(URL.createObjectURL(file)); a.addEventListener('loadedmetadata', ...): if a.duration > 61 → showToast('เสียงยาวเกิน 1 นาที กรุณาเลือกไฟล์สั้นกว่านี้','error') + reject. If a.duration is Infinity/NaN (some m4a) → accept with provisional durationSec=60 (the viewer's loadedmetadata reconciles if finite). Round to integer.
  - setAudioBlob(file); setAudioMime(file.type||''); setAudioSource('upload'); audioPreviewUrl set; setRecMode('recorded').

PREVIEW / RE-RECORD (recMode==='recorded' OR editing with existing audioUrl):
  - Render the SAME <VoicePlayer> the viewer uses (single source of truth → creator hears exactly what recipient gets), fed { audioUrl: audioPreviewUrl||audioUrl, durationSec: audioDuration, mimeType: audioMime, themeColors, caption: voiceCaption }.
  - Button 'อัดใหม่' (record source) / 'เลือกไฟล์ใหม่' (upload source): revoke audioObjectUrlRef, clear audioBlob, setRecMode('idle'); when editing existing audioUrl with no new blob, also clear audioUrl so submit re-validates.
  - Caption input: reuse the exact image caption block (label 'คำบรรยาย (ไม่จำเป็น)', placeholder 'เช่น "ฟังเสียงนี้นะ..."', className input-valentine, maxLength STORY_TEXT_LIMITS.caption, with the existing CharCount component).

DENIED RECOVERY (recMode==='denied'): a card — 'คุณปิดการเข้าถึงไมโครโฟนไว้ — เปิดสิทธิ์ในการตั้งค่าเบราว์เซอร์แล้วลองใหม่ หรืออัปโหลดไฟล์เสียงแทน' + button 'ลองอีกครั้ง' (re-request getUserMedia) + the upload input stays visible. Never a dead-end.

SUBMIT (new case 'voice' in handleSubmit switch ~line 191):
  if (!audioBlob && !audioUrl) { showToast('กรุณาอัดเสียงหรืออัปโหลดไฟล์เสียงก่อน','error'); return; }
  let finalAudioUrl = audioUrl;
  if (audioBlob) {
    try { setUploading(true); finalAudioUrl = await uploadAudio(audioBlob, audioMime); }
    catch { showToast('อัปโหลดเสียงไม่สำเร็จ กรุณาลองใหม่อีกครั้ง','error'); setUploading(false); return; }
    finally { setUploading(false); }
  }
  const durationSec = Math.min(60, Math.max(1, Math.round(audioDuration)));
  story = { ...baseStory, type:'voice', content:{ audioUrl: finalAudioUrl, durationSec, mimeType: audioMime, source: audioSource, caption: voiceCaption.trim() || undefined } };
  break;
  (Submit button reuses existing disabled={uploading} + 'กำลังอัพโหลด...' label at line 657.)

CLEANUP: extend the existing unmount useEffect (StoryEditor.tsx:142-148) to ALSO: revoke audioObjectUrlRef.current; mediaStreamRef.current?.getTracks().forEach(t=>t.stop()); clearInterval(recTimerRef.current). Prevents a hot mic.

A11y: record orb aria-label 'อัดเสียง'; timer span aria-live='polite'; live region announces 'กำลังอัดเสียง' / 'อัดเสียงเสร็จแล้ว'.

ALSO UPDATE src/components/StoryList.tsx getStoryPreview (line 25-40): add `case 'voice': return story.content.caption || 'ข้อความเสียง';` before default — without it the list shows a blank preview (default returns '').
voiceSpec.viewerSpec:
NEW FILE: src/components/VoicePlayer.tsx — SHARED by the editor preview AND the StoryViewer (one implementation, WYSIWYG). NO autoplay. PROPS: { audioUrl: string; durationSec: number; mimeType?: string; caption?: string; themeColors?: ThemeColors; onEnded?: () => void }. STORYVIEWER WIRING — add `case 'voice'` to the switch in src/components/StoryViewer.tsx (after 'scratch', ~line 118), same card shell as image: case 'voice': return (
); (onReveal here is page.tsx's generic `() => setRevealedStories(prev => new Set(prev).add(id))` — see autoAdvanceSemantics for why reveal is wired but does NOT trigger auto-advance.) NO-AUTOPLAY POLICY (HARD RULE): the

slideshowSpec.viewerSpec:
NEW FILE: src/components/SlideshowViewer.tsx — adopts ScratchCard's EXACT prop contract so it slots into StoryViewer + page.tsx's reveal machine with ZERO new page state. PROPS (mirror ScratchCard): { imageUrls: string[]; themeColors?: ThemeColors; initialRevealed?: boolean; onComplete?: () => void; caption?: string }. STORYVIEWER WIRING — add `case 'slideshow'` (shaped like the scratch case at StoryViewer.tsx:102-118): case 'slideshow': return (
); (onReveal is page.tsx's generic reveal handler — identical wiring to scratch. NO page.tsx change beyond the shouldAutoAdvance predicate; see autoAdvanceSemantics.) INTERNAL STATE: index (0-based current slide); hasReachedEnd (bool); paused (bool, true once user takes manual control). On mount, if initialRevealed → hasReachedEnd=true (re-visits don't re-gate). Component is keyed by story.id in StoryViewer (page.tsx:452 key={currentStory.id}) so handleReplay's currentIndex=0 + revealedStories reset remounts it fresh. STAGE: rounded-2xl overflow-hidden frame, aspect-[4/5] (portrait-friendly for phone photos), max-h-[500px], themed bg. Slides absolutely stacked; only current is opacity-100, others opacity-0 with a 500ms transition-opacity CROSSFADE. object-cover so each photo fills edge-to-edge (cinematic). Mark the stage div with data-interactive (page.tsx:173 already skips it) so the page tap-nav never hijacks slideshow gestures. First slide uses ImageWithLoader for the themed heart-loader shimmer; subsequent slides plain but PRELOAD: on entering slide i, `new Image().src = imageUrls[i+1]` so the next crossfade is instant on slow networks. KEN BURNS (pure CSS, NEW keyframes in globals.css): the active slide's runs animation 'ken-burns-a|b|c' cycled by index%3 (scale 1→1.10 + small translate, alternating transform-origin per variant for variety), duration ~4.6s ease-out, re-applied via a React key on the active so it restarts cleanly each slide. @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) → animation:none (keep crossfade only, or instant swap). AUTO-PLAY: internal setTimeout advances index every DWELL=3500ms while the slideshow is the active story AND !paused. Advance ONLY up to the last slide (does NOT loop). On reaching the LAST slide: stop the timer, fire onComplete?.() EXACTLY ONCE (guard with hasReachedEnd ref) → adds story id to revealedStories. A small celebratory pulse on the active dot when last slide reached (reuse animate-pulse-heart timing) — the 'that was the album' beat before the reveal→auto-advance handoff. TOP SEGMENTED MINI-PROGRESS (Instagram-stories language, graft from ux-polish): a row of n thin bars at the TOP of the stage; bars < index fully filled themeColors.primary; the current bar fills over DWELL via a CSS scaleX animation; bars > index empty `${dark}18`. This telegraphs slide dwell. (This is SLIDE progress, distinct from the page's STORY-level segmented top bar.) DOTS: a centered row of n dots BELOW the stage (gap-1.5). Active dot = elongated pill themeColors.primary (slightly wider); inactive = `${themeColors.dark}25` circles. Dots are
slideshowSpec.uploadSpec:
NO new upload function. Slideshow images go through the EXISTING uploadImage(file) (src/lib/upload.ts) → processImage resize to max 1200x1200 + WebP 0.85 → `images` bucket under the existing `images/` prefix → public URL. Slideshow is literally N images, so it reuses 100% of the proven pipeline and stays format-consistent (WebP) with all other story images.

ORCHESTRATION lives in the SlideshowField editor (NOT a new lib fn — keeps upload.ts pure/stateless like the single-purpose uploadImage): bounded-concurrency(2) promise pool over the not-yet-uploaded slides, with per-SlideItem.uploadedUrl caching so a 3/5 partial failure retries ONLY the failed two and never writes a half-list to the DB. The 'กำลังอัปโหลด n/N' counter increments as each resolves.

Optional thin convenience (only if desired): export async function uploadImages(files: File[]): Promise<string[]> { return Promise.all(files.map(f => uploadImage(f))); } — but the editor must STILL own SlideItem state for retry caching, so this helper is non-essential; prefer the editor-owned pool.

MIME/SIZE: rely on accept='image/*'; runtime pre-check file.type.startsWith('image/') + size<=10MB (IMAGE_MAX_BYTES=10*1024*1024 in constants); processImage's img.onerror already rejects non-images.

ORPHAN POLICY: same as existing image flow — uploads at submit, before onSave; abandoning after partial success orphans those `images/` objects. Never a half-list to the DB. Future GC cron out of scope for v1.

finalSpec.migrationSql:
-- supabase/migrations/021-add-voice-and-slideshow-story-types.sql
-- Migration: Add 'voice' and 'slideshow' story types + a dedicated public `audio` bucket.
-- 'voice'     : a recorded/uploaded audio message (<= 60s, <= 10MB), played in the viewer.
-- 'slideshow' : 2..5 images shown as an auto-playing Ken Burns slideshow.
-- stories.content is the existing jsonb column (database.ts: stories.content is Json), so
-- NO column/type schema change is needed — only the type CHECK constraint is widened
-- (same additive pattern as 009-add-scratch-story-type.sql / 010-add-question-story-type.sql).
-- Slideshow images REUSE the existing `images` bucket via uploadImage (no storage change for slideshow).

-- ============================================================
-- 1) Widen the stories type CHECK constraint (drop + re-add)
-- ============================================================
ALTER TABLE public.stories DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS stories_type_check;

ALTER TABLE public.stories ADD CONSTRAINT stories_type_check
  CHECK (type IN ('password', 'image', 'text', 'text-image', 'youtube', 'scratch', 'question', 'voice', 'slideshow'));

-- ============================================================
-- 2) Dedicated PUBLIC `audio` bucket for voice notes
--    (mirrors the cartoon-images precedent in 012-add-cartoon-generations.sql)
--    NOTE: we deliberately do NOT reuse the `images` bucket — its RLS/MIME were set
--    in the Supabase dashboard and are unverifiable from code; a dedicated bucket with
--    its own `voices/` folder INSERT policy is the only safe choice.
-- ============================================================
INSERT INTO storage.buckets (id, name, public)
VALUES ('audio', 'audio', true)
ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;

-- Optional hard cap (defense in depth; the editor's 60s/10MB checks are the real gate).
-- NOTE: allowed_mime_types is intentionally LEFT OUT here. A codec-suffixed container
-- ('audio/mp4;codecs=...') or an empty-MIME iOS file can be wrongly rejected by a bucket
-- MIME allowlist; uploadAudio strips ';codecs=...' before sending contentType, but the
-- safest default is to enforce mime ONLY editor-side. If your Supabase version errors on
-- the file_size_limit column, this single statement can be removed without affecting the feature.
UPDATE storage.buckets
SET file_size_limit = 10485760  -- 10 MB
WHERE id = 'audio';

-- Public read (audio is shared via public memory URLs)
CREATE POLICY "Public read audio"
  ON storage.objects FOR SELECT
  USING (bucket_id = 'audio');

-- Authenticated users may upload voice notes into the `voices/` folder
-- (browser uploads use the anon/auth client; memory creators are authenticated owners).
CREATE POLICY "Users can upload voices"
  ON storage.objects FOR INSERT
  WITH CHECK (
    bucket_id = 'audio'
    AND (storage.foldername(name))[1] = 'voices'
    AND auth.role() = 'authenticated'
  );

-- Service role full access (API routes / future GC cron)
CREATE POLICY "Service role full access audio"
  ON storage.objects FOR ALL
  USING (bucket_id = 'audio')
  WITH CHECK (bucket_id = 'audio');

-- ============================================================
-- TS-only follow-ups (NOT part of this migration; src/types/database.ts needs no edit
-- because stories.type is `string` and stories.content is `Json`):
--   - src/types/memory.ts: extend StoryType union + add VoiceStory/SlideshowStory + extend MemoryStory union
-- ============================================================

fileChanges.spec: New migration file with the SQL in migrationSql. Widens stories_type_check to include 'voice' and 'slideshow'; creates the public `audio` bucket with public-read + voices/-folder authenticated INSERT + service-role ALL policies (mirrors 012); sets file_size_limit 10MB; omits allowed_mime_types by design. Run after 020.

Line 1: widen StoryType to add '| voice | slideshow'. After QuestionStory (~line 48): add `export interface VoiceStory extends BaseStory { type:'voice'; content:{ audioUrl:string; durationSec:number; mimeType:string; source:'record'|'upload'; caption?:string } }` and `export interface SlideshowStory extends BaseStory { type:'slideshow'; content:{ imageUrls:string[]; caption?:string } }`. Line 50: add `| VoiceStory | SlideshowStory` to the MemoryStory union.

Add after CARTOON_CREDIT_COST block: `export const VOICE_MAX_DURATION_SEC = 60;` `export const VOICE_MAX_SIZE_BYTES = 10 * 1024 * 1024;` `export const VOICE_ACCEPTED_EXT = /\.(mp3|m4a|wav|aac|ogg|webm)$/i;` `export const SLIDESHOW_MIN_IMAGES = 2;` `export const SLIDESHOW_MAX_IMAGES = 5;` `export const IMAGE_MAX_BYTES = 10 * 1024 * 1024;`. Reuse existing STORY_TEXT_LIMITS.caption (80) for both new captions.

Add `export async function uploadAudio(blob: Blob, mimeType: string): Promise<string>` exactly as in voiceSpec.uploadSpec: guard `if (!supabase) throw ...` (matches existing pattern — file imports { supabase } from './supabase'); 10MB size guard; strip ';codecs=...' to baseMime; map baseMime→ext (mp4/x-m4a/aac→m4a, mpeg→mp3, webm→webm, ogg→ogg, wav/x-wav→wav, default webm); filePath `voices/${Date.now()}-${rand}.${ext}`; supabase.storage.from('audio').upload(filePath, blob, { contentType: baseMime||'audio/webm', cacheControl:'3600' }); throw on error; return getPublicUrl. NO transcoding.
New shared component (editor preview + viewer). Props { audioUrl, durationSec, mimeType?, caption?, themeColors?, onEnded? }. No-autoplay gesture-gated

New viewer component adopting ScratchCard's prop contract: { imageUrls, themeColors?, initialRevealed?, onComplete?, caption? }. aspect-[4/5] max-h-500px stage (data-interactive), stacked slides with 500ms crossfade + object-cover. Ken Burns via ken-burns-a/b/c cycled index%3 (re-keyed per slide). Auto-play setTimeout DWELL=3500ms up to last slide only (no loop); onComplete fires ONCE on last slide (hasReachedEnd guard) + celebratory dot pulse. Top segmented mini-progress (per-slide dwell). Dot row below (buttons, aria-label, data-interactive) jump+pause. Swipe(>40px)/tap-zone(30/70) self-contained with stopPropagation (never bubbles to story nav); manual sets paused. First slide ImageWithLoader; preload next via new Image(). Caption italic below. role=group, aria-roledescription, aria-live announcements. prefers-reduced-motion disables motion.

`; case 'slideshow' → `
`.

EDIT shouldAutoAdvance (lines 149-153) ONLY. Add voice to the youtube-style exclusion AND slideshow to the scratch-style reveal-gate: `currentStory.type !== 'youtube' && currentStory.type !== 'voice' && !(currentStory.type === 'scratch' && !revealedStories.has(currentStory.id)) && !(currentStory.type === 'slideshow' && !revealedStories.has(currentStory.id))`. No other change: voice/slideshow already render via StoryViewer; the generic onReveal at line 456 already adds to revealedStories (used by slideshow); handleReplay (line 233) already resets revealedStories. The story-content wrapper max-width (line 440) currently special-cases youtube to max-w-4xl — leave as-is; voice uses max-w-md and slideshow max-w-2xl inside their own cards so the default max-w-2xl wrapper is fine.

(storyTypeLabels/storyTypeIcons auto-include the new types via the StoryEditor registry import, so the list icon+label render automatically.)

Add after auto-advance-fill (line 112): three Ken Burns keyframes `@keyframes ken-burns-a { from{transform:scale(1) translate(0,0); transform-origin:top left} to{transform:scale(1.10) translate(-2%,-2%)} }`, `ken-burns-b` (origin center, scale to 1.10 translate(2%,-1%)), `ken-burns-c` (origin bottom right, scale to 1.10 translate(-1%,2%)), each used with duration ~4.6s ease-out forwards via inline style/class on the active slide img. Add a play-aura keyframe `@keyframes voice-aura { 0%,100%{box-shadow:0 0 0 0 var(--aura,rgba(0,0,0,0.12))} 50%{box-shadow:0 0 0 14px transparent} }` and a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce){ .ken-burns-a,.ken-burns-b,.ken-burns-c{animation:none!important} .voice-aura{animation:none!important} }` block. (Reuse existing animate-pulse-heart for the recording orb + last-slide dot pulse — no new keyframe needed there.)

finalSpec.autoAdvanceSemantics:
The viewer's only auto-advance/reveal driver is `shouldAutoAdvance` (page.tsx:149-153), the 5s timer effect (156-164), and the `revealedStories` Set (toggled by StoryViewer's `onReveal`). Both new types plug into this with a SINGLE one-line predicate edit and ZERO new page state.

VOICE — youtube-style exclusion (CONFLICT RESOLVED in favor of the simpler option). Add `&& currentStory.type !== 'voice'` to shouldAutoAdvance. A voice story therefore NEVER 5s-auto-advances (a timer would cut a loved one off mid-sentence); the recipient finishes listening and taps Next / the right tap-zone themselves — identical to the existing youtube behavior. Its segmented top progress bar shows the static no-fill state (transform: scaleX(0)), same as youtube. NOTE on the wired onReveal: StoryViewer passes `onEnded={onReveal}` to VoicePlayer, so when audio finishes the story IS added to revealedStories — but because voice is excluded from shouldAutoAdvance entirely, reveal does NOT trigger advancement; it is harmless bookkeeping (and keeps replay/back consistent). We deliberately reject ux-polish's 'reveal-on-end → handleNext after 900ms grace' because (a) it requires new page.tsx navigation logic and (b) the `onended` event is flaky on iOS if the audio errors mid-stream, so relying on it to advance would risk a stuck story. Manual Next is always available and predictable.

SLIDESHOW — scratch-style reveal-gate. Add `&& !(currentStory.type === 'slideshow' && !revealedStories.has(currentStory.id))` to shouldAutoAdvance. While the recipient is still cycling slides (story not yet revealed) the STORY does NOT 5s-auto-advance and the page's segmented top bar shows no auto-fill. SlideshowViewer auto-plays slides every 3500ms internally; on reaching the LAST slide it fires `onComplete()` exactly once (hasReachedEnd guard), which is StoryViewer's generic onReveal `() => setRevealedStories(prev => new Set(prev).add(id))` (page.tsx:456) — adding the id flips shouldAutoAdvance to true, and the standard 5s timer + auto-advance-fill animation then carry the recipient to the NEXT story. This is the exact scratch contract (page.tsx:153). Two SEPARATE progress concepts coexist cleanly: the page's segmented top bar = STORY progress (untouched); SlideshowViewer's own top mini-segments + dot row = SLIDE progress. handleReplay (page.tsx:233) clears revealedStories and resets currentIndex=0; since StoryViewer keys the child by story.id (page.tsx:452), SlideshowViewer remounts fresh at slide 0 on replay, re-gating correctly. Manual swipe/tap inside the stage is fully self-contained (stopPropagation) and reaching the last slide manually also fires onComplete once.

GESTURE COEXISTENCE: page.tsx handleContentTap (167-185) ignores `button, a, input, ..., canvas, video, [data-interactive]` (line 173, verified). VoicePlayer's play button + range scrubber + download link, and SlideshowViewer's stage + dots, all carry data-interactive (or are real button/input/a), so the page tap-nav never swallows their gestures and intra-slideshow swipes never skip a whole story.
openQuestions: Audio bucket file_size_limit column: the migration UPDATE sets file_size_limit=10485760 on the `audio` bucket. On hosted Supabase this is supported, but if the project ever runs an older self-hosted version that lacks the column, that single statement must be removed (it is isolated and commented). Confirm the hosted plan accepts it (expected yes).

Verify before/after deploy that the dedicated `audio` bucket's authenticated-INSERT policy is satisfied by the memory creator's browser session. Creators are authenticated owners (memories CRUD is owner-gated and uploadImage already uploads from the browser successfully), so auth.role()='authenticated' should hold — but a quick smoke test of an actual voice upload from a logged-in session is recommended since this is a NEW bucket/policy.

DWELL timing (3500ms/slide) and Ken Burns scale (1.10) are chosen for feel; product may want to tune. They are constants local to SlideshowViewer, not stored in content (the SlideshowStory.durationMs field from ux-polish was intentionally dropped to keep content minimal and the editor light — re-add only if per-story tuning becomes a requirement).

Cross-browser audio decode residual risk (accepted for v1, no fix shipped): a webm-opus clip recorded on desktop Chrome and opened on a very old iOS Safari without opus support won't play inline. Mitigations in place: each device records the format it can play, the <source type> is correct, and the error state offers a 'เปิดไฟล์เสียง' download link. A future server-side transcode-to-mp3 is out of scope (would need a new dependency).

Orphaned storage objects (audio + slideshow images) when a creator uploads then abandons the editor — same pre-existing behavior as the single-image flow. No GC in v1; the voices/ folder + images/ layout is chosen so a future Vercel-cron sweep (cross-checking stories.content via service role) is trivial. Confirm this deferral is acceptable.

In-app webview (LINE/Facebook/Messenger) coverage: capability detection hides the record orb and forces the upload path with a Thai note. A rare residual edge exists where a restrictive webview also blocks the file picker, leaving the user unable to add voice in that context — acceptable for v1, but worth confirming LINE's in-app browser permits <input type=file> for audio (it generally does).
